message:
refactor(fcp-core): improve sort idioms and duration consistency

Cleanup pass on fcp-core to use more idiomatic Rust patterns and
ensure consistent duration representations.

checkpoint.rs:
- Replaces manual closure sort with sort_by_key using std::cmp::Reverse
- Before: ranked.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0))
- After:  ranked.sort_by_key(|item| std::cmp::Reverse(item.0))
- More readable and avoids manual comparison ordering

error.rs:
- Changes retry_after test assertion from Duration::from_millis(5000)
  to Duration::from_secs(5)
- Both are equivalent but from_secs(5) is clearer for whole-second
  values

lease.rs:
- Same sort_by_key idiom improvement as checkpoint.rs
- Descending HRW hash sort for node ranking

These changes are purely stylistic improvements with no behavioral
impact, following Rust API guidelines for sort operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -346,7 +346,7 @@ pub fn rank_checkpoint_coordinators(
         .iter()
         .map(|node| (hrw_hash_checkpoint(zone_id, epoch, node), node.clone()))
         .collect();
-    ranked.sort_by(|a, b| b.0.cmp(&a.0)); // Descending by hash
+    ranked.sort_by_key(|item| std::cmp::Reverse(item.0)); // Descending by hash
     ranked.into_iter().map(|(_, node)| node).collect()
 }
